flashcards.definition: Zeros of the numerator (where expression = 0) and zeros of the denominator (where expression is undefined).
A number line divided at critical numbers; test the sign of the expression in each interval.
For ≤ or ≥, include x-values where the expression equals 0 (numerator = 0).
Never include x-values where the denominator = 0 (always excluded).
Combine terms so one side is 0 and the other is a single rational expression.
